oai_dc:dc.dc:title: A systems approach to nursing calf pneumonia
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Vander Ley, Brian
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: systems thinking
nursing calf pneumonia
summer pneumonia
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Systems thinking (ST) offers a holistic, problem-oriented frame­work for understanding and solving deeply entrenched prob­lems in complex systems. Basic to ST is the understanding that system behavior, including problems, is the result of the com­ponents of a system and the relationships between those com­ponents. Fundamental ST concepts include the Iceberg Frame­work, feedback, system homeostasis, mental models, Causal Loop Diagrams and System Archetypes. When applied to nursing calf pneumonia (NCP), ST underscores the ongoing difficulty in understanding root causes of disease. Systems thinking prompts us to ask crucial “why” questions that address our inability to make progress toward a solution and encourages us to discover our own contributions to the problem and use that knowledge to find leverage to effectively change the system.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 21-23
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
